question:
Read the passage carefully and answer the questions that follow.
Developments in electronic science have transformed the art of record-keeping in the modern age. Traditionally records of events were kept only in people's minds. It depended very much on the rententive power of the human memory. This was extremely dangerous as people either forgot events wholly or in part, or deliberately falsified details to suit their various interests. Interminable arguments were thus the order of the day. Even writing which replaced mental recording was not entirely free from these shortcomings as untruths could be written as true records either willingly or inadvertently. With the advent of the electronic memory, however, these dangers now show not only what happened, but also who did or said what, including how and when.
The author believes that electronic recording is
options: inferior to both mental recording and writing
superior to both mental recording and writing

question:
Choose the option that best explains the sentence:
You are driving too fast for safety.
options: That speed is all right and safe
That speed is not fast enough for safety
That speed is not entirely safe
You should drive faster to ensure safety

question:
Choose the option that best explains the sentence:
For all I care, the man may be dead.
options: I am not sure the man is dead
I am not interested in his death
I very much care in case he is dead
I am ignorant of the man's death

question:
Select the option opposite in meaning to the underlined word:
The priest was invited to <u>consecrate</u> the new building

Select the option opposite in meaning to the underlined word:
A majority of those who sat for the last jamb examination are <u>sanguine</u> of success

When we woke up this morning, the sky was <u>overcast</u>

Select the option nearest in meaning to the underlined word:
Enemies of progress <u>covertly</u> strive to undermine the efforts of this administration

The boy is constantly under some ______ that he is the best student in the class.

Choose the word that best completes the sentence:

Choose the word or phrase that best completes the sentence:

Choose the option that best completes the sentence:
Young men should not get mixed ______ politics.
